plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and positions below stop.
Reduce SMH to 5% and CRWD to 10% to de-risk from downtrends and concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income-focused, stable exposure.
Allocate 15% of the book to cash for capital preservation and buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expected swings are much smaller, but growth potential is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reduce overall leverage.
Reduce SMH to 8% to decrease exposure to a downtrending sector.
Buy GOOGL and AMZN, allocating 10% to each, leveraging strong scanner setups.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and diversifies into quality tech. While still exposed to market swings, the risk profile is more moderate than aggressive.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate capital.
Reduce SMH to 5% to decrease exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase QQQ to 25% for aggressive broad tech exposure.
Buy GOOGL at 15% and AMZN at 10%, leveraging high-scoring scanner setups.
Maintain a small 5% cash buffer, focusing on deploying capital.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, stretching for growth. It leans into high-scoring long setups and broad tech, accepting higher leverage. This mode carries significantly higher drawdown risk, especially with leveraged ETFs' decay, and requires strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, SMH, and CRWD to eliminate leveraged, high-beta, and downtrending holdings.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize exposure to income-generating ETFs.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and stability.
This plan focuses on steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income plan aims for approximately 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions. It significantly reduces high-beta and leveraged exposure, focusing on stability. While designed for lower drawdown and reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
